---
title: "Week 8 - gene flow"
---

## Overview
In today's practical, we will use a [published](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-020-61560-8) microsatellite dataset on Antarctic fur seals to investigate gene flow and population differentiation. 

## Exercise 1

```{r}
library(adegenet)

data <- read.structure(
  "../data/week_8_migration/structure_in_gaz.stru",
  n.ind =  2000, 
  n.loc = 39, 
  onerowperind = FALSE,
  col.lab = 1,
  col.pop = 2,
  NA.char = "-9",
  row.marknames = 0,
  col.others = NULL,
  pop = NULL, sep = NULL, ask = F, quiet = FALSE
)

head(seal)
nInd(seal)
nLoc(seal)
pop(seal)

tab(seal, freq=TRUE) #af

library(hierfstat)

basic.stats(seal)

#Which populations are most genetically diverse?

library(hierfstat)

fst <- pairwise.WCfst(data)
fst

# which pop strongest genetic isolation

pca <- dudi.pca(tab(data, NA.method="mean"), 
                scale=FALSE, 
                scannf=FALSE, 
                nf=3)

s.class(pca$li, pop(data))

# connected or isolated?
# fsta match pca?
# are there migrants?
```
